fields.exact_resume_goal: Review promoted idea and decide whether to execute, refine, or retire it.
fields.scope_constraints: Operator observation: in two fresh chat sessions, immediately after the operator provided operational status in response to the readiness prompt, Gemini emitted a large raw JSON readiness block and only then produced the user-facing response. The JSON included readiness_confirmed, tool availability, missing_minimum_evidence, known_scope_defaults, and related internal structured state. Desired behavior: once the environment is confirmed, Gemini should emit only the final operator-facing triage response and begin working the alert without exposing internal JSON or scaffolding.
fields.plan_title: Promoted idea: Gemini emits large readiness JSON block after environment confirmation in new sessions
fields.next_recommended_action: Review this promoted idea-derived plan and break it into Work Items if still useful.
fields.why_current_task_matters: This matters because it is a repeated operator-surface leakage defect on fresh-session startup, breaks usability, and confirms the startup path still leaks internal structure even after the environment is known.

fields.exact_resume_goal: Review promoted idea and decide whether to execute, refine, or retire it.
fields.scope_constraints: Add a reusable Gemini workflow rule for report-driven IOC expansion/removal: when the analyst uploads incident documents and asks Gemini to add missing IOCs or remove incident-localized ones, Gemini must enumerate the files it actually reviewed, distinguish analyst-supplied IOCs from report-derived IOCs, and give short provenance notes for each added or removed indicator family. If only a subset of attachments was reviewed, Gemini should say so plainly instead of implying full review coverage.
fields.plan_title: Promoted idea: Gemini attachment review must state exact IOC provenance
fields.next_recommended_action: Review this promoted idea-derived plan and break it into Work Items if still useful.
fields.why_current_task_matters: This transcript showed a support-proof gap: analyst note said 5 documents were attached, but Gemini claimed review while naming only 3 and then changed the IOC set without visible per-file grounding. Preserving this as a durable rule helps future Gemini evidence review and repair work.

fields.exact_resume_goal: Review promoted idea and decide whether to execute, refine, or retire it.
fields.scope_constraints: Create an Airtable pattern where a specific lifecycle/delete field value on a row triggers an Airtable automation that deletes that same row. This closes the assistant-side delete_records gap by letting ChatGPT mark records for deletion through ordinary update operations while Airtable performs the actual deletion through an owner-configured automation.
fields.plan_title: Promoted idea: Create Airtable row-delete trigger automation
fields.next_recommended_action: Review this promoted idea-derived plan and break it into Work Items if still useful.
fields.why_current_task_matters: This reduces manual cleanup friction during schema resets, stale-row cleanup, duplicate-task cleanup, and future large Airtable migrations where the connector can update records but cannot delete records directly.

Operator corrected bundle hygiene rule: do not include ChatGPT-only artifacts in GitHub Desktop bundles. Startup attachments still say automatic startup should avoid rendering Airtable UI, which remains acceptable for startup, but active execution should allow displays when useful.

fields.exact_resume_goal: Review promoted idea and decide whether to execute, refine, or retire it.
fields.scope_constraints: Add a durable Gemini validation/repair rule that when analyst-run KQL/ESQL/osquery steps return no results, Gemini must treat that as bounded absence rather than converting it into proof of stealth, malware, or collapse of the benign hypothesis. Require the response to explicitly separate: what the alert directly proves, what the no-result query fails to add, and what coverage limitations could still explain the gap.
fields.plan_title: Promoted idea: Gemini no-results honesty gate
fields.next_recommended_action: Review this promoted idea-derived plan and break it into Work Items if still useful.
fields.why_current_task_matters: This transcript shows a reusable failure pattern: Gemini escalated from negative telemetry into strong maliciousness language and a collector pivot without enough direct evidence. Preserving the rule will help future repair and validation work stay evidence-first.

fields.exact_resume_goal: Review promoted idea and decide whether to execute, refine, or retire it.
fields.scope_constraints: Add a durable Gemini validation/repair rule that any emitted Elastic KQL or ES|QL intended for operator execution must be parser-safe before being presented as ready. After the first operator-reported parser failure, Gemini should switch into bounded syntax-repair mode: identify the exact failing token class, make one disciplined correction, and avoid fresh unsupported changes such as slash-substitution for Windows paths unless explicitly grounded. Do not claim the query is fixed until the operator confirms parse success or the model explicitly states it is still unverified.
fields.plan_title: Promoted idea: Gemini SIEM query parser-safety gate
fields.next_recommended_action: Review this promoted idea-derived plan and break it into Work Items if still useful.
fields.why_current_task_matters: This transcript shows repeated correction loops wasted analyst time and eroded trust even though the later tiered-hunt strategy was directionally better. Preserving the rule in Airtable keeps future Gemini repair/review work from depending on chat memory.

If the model needs internal structuring, that structure must remain hidden and the first visible answer should already be in the normal operator format.

Preserving the rule prevents future work from depending on chat memory.

fields.exact_resume_goal: Define signature rules for duplicate review.
fields.scope_constraints: Design and execute a fully decomposed Airtable database redesign plan that improves searchability, filtering, standardization, deduplication prevention, schema clarity, operational usefulness, and support for DCOIR collector / Gemini agent deliverables. Start from validated read-only full export/profile. Use aggressive cleanup and normalization recommendations, but pause before destructive or high-impact schema/data actions with exact approval packets. Preserve Delete Queue automation semantics and exclude Delete Queue from broad plan-scope analysis unless explicitly requested.
fields.plan_title: MAKE THE AIRTABLE DATABASE BETTER DESIGNED
fields.why_current_task_matters: This plan creates the durable execution structure for database redesign before mutations, so later batches cannot hand-wave field conversion, table replacement, select-option normalization, dedupe, backfill, or destructive approval gates.
fields.resume_detail: Validated export/profile: chatgpt-exec request dcoir-db-redesign-full-export-profile-20260508-1835z, FullRecords, 21 tables, 1270 records, 308 fields, no script-level error report. Current next step: use DCOIR Cleanup WBS as a temporary empty planning surface, then make replacement with a generic DCOIR Execution WBS table an early design/schema task.

fields.exact_resume_goal: Review promoted idea and decide whether to execute, refine, or retire it.
fields.scope_constraints: During live Gemini governed-source repair, a GitHub `update_file` attempt failed with a 409 SHA mismatch because the file had advanced beyond the earlier fetched snapshot. The correct recovery was not to treat this as a mysterious blocker. The right bounded recovery is: refetch the exact live file from `main`, take the new blob SHA as authoritative, rebase the prepared edit onto that current content, and retry the write against the refreshed SHA. Treat this as a normal connector write-race or stale-snapshot condition rather than a workflow derailment.
fields.plan_title: Promoted idea: GitHub update_file SHA mismatch should trigger immediate live-state refetch and reapply, not confusion or delay
fields.next_recommended_action: Review this promoted idea-derived plan and break it into Work Items if still useful.
fields.why_current_task_matters: This is a reusable GitHub connector blocker-recovery lesson for governed existing-file updates. Capturing it should reduce future hesitation, prevent stale-sha confusion, and support faster recovery in grouped GitHub write lanes.
